strengths: High intelligibility and clear articulation, ensuring effective listener comprehension.

areas_for_improvement: Reduce reliance on filler words such as 'actually' and 'like' to improve the pacing of delivery.
Incorporate greater prosodic variation and voice modulation to enhance engagement and maintain audience attention.
Expand the repertoire of advanced discourse markers to move beyond basic sequencing and improve narrative flow.
executive_summary: The candidate demonstrates strong technical proficiency and clear pronunciation, making them highly intelligible in a professional setting. While their vocabulary is well-suited to the role, they need to focus on increasing their speaking rate and reducing conversational fillers to improve overall fluency. By incorporating more varied voice modulation and complex discourse structures, the candidate will be better equipped to deliver impactful and persuasive presentations. Overall, the candidate shows good potential with a solid foundation in professional communication.

The pronoun 'this' should be pluralized to 'these' to match the plural noun 'applications'.

features.raw_text: Hi, I'm Rasika and I am currently working as a GENAI QA engineer at Capgemini and I work mostly across GENAI initiatives and currently working across conversational banking and AI domain. So I have worked on two of their most popular GenAI initiatives around which first one is a AI powered letter generation application and second one is a customer helper brag model. So I'm mostly working around testing this genAI applications right from APIs testing to LLM response testing with the help of open source evaluation frameworks like DP val and evaluating the LLM responses with the help of metrics, predefined metrics like context relevance, see precision context, precision hallucination, toxicity, bias etc. So and comparing those So I secured 93% in 10th standard, 76.15% in 12th standard and in graduation 7.7 CGPA. Yes, it is a four-year technical degree. Yeah, so I have been working on writing SQL queries to fetch the data from databases and validating the model behavior responses and checking if if if all the data that is being given to the LLM and the data that is getting generated, the generated letter and all the inputs like complaint points and all are being properly saved in the database. So yes, I'm mostly working around writing SQL queries and other manual testing practices in my current role. So, I am comfortable working from 4:00 PM to 1:00 AM shift. So, I have been working around automation testing where I have been using Python with with BDD for writing test scripts for API automation and other validation for APIs. So, we are using Python plus BDD for our QA automation testing. So, I have been working as a GenAI QA engineer right from the start. So Capgemini is my first company and I'm working as a QA automation there right from the start. So, yeah, I actually I was working as a front-end developer at my internship at Acyam. So, and after that I got an offer letter of Capgemini for which I switched to Capgemini as it was a full-time opportunity. So I'm working here for 2.5 years plus years now and currently I want to switch because of career growth and to develop more and more skills in my current role.
